name: Win-WLAN-Audit-Tool

documentation.readme: 📌 Project Overview The Win-WLAN-Audit-Tool is a specialized post-exploitation security script designed for Windows environments. It demonstrates the ease with which stored wireless credentials can be retrieved by an authorized user or an attacker who has gained local access to a system.
usage_sections: Automated Enumeration: Scans the host system to identify all saved WiFi profiles using the Windows netsh utility.
Credential Extraction: Force-reveals Pre-Shared Keys (PSK) in cleartext for all identified profiles.
Language-Agnostic Parsing: Employs advanced Regular Expressions (Regex) to ensure compatibility across different Windows system languages.
Security Reporting: Displays results in a clean, structured table for quick auditing.

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

arguments.name: --evil-twin
arguments.description: Spawn an Evil Twin access point with the same SSID.

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

function_signatures.name: scan
function_signatures.description: Perform a read-only scan / probe of the target.

function_signatures.name: exploit
function_signatures.description: Run the offensive step against the target. Returns ok, data, error envelope.

function_signatures.name: report
function_signatures.description: Render the result into a human-readable or machine-readable report.

function_signatures.name: validate
function_signatures.description: Pre-flight check: confirm the target is reachable and the tool can run.

function_signatures.name: cleanup
function_signatures.description: Remove any artifacts left behind by the tool (pcaps, sessions, files).

function_signatures.name: chain
function_signatures.description: Take a previous tool's result and feed it as input to this tool.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with worknshubh/wifi-sukuna (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with tdiprima/wifi-is-radio (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with t6x/reaver-wps-fork-t6x (score=10); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['wifi_2_4_ghz', 'wifi_5_ghz', 'shell_linux'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 4: same attack_surface=['wifi_2_4_ghz', 'wifi_5_ghz', 'shell_linux'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 5: same attack_surface=['wifi_2_4_ghz', 'wifi_5_ghz', 'shell_linux'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [wifi_2_4_ghz, wifi_5_ghz, shell_linux] in the Wireless category. Fits when the engagement needs: 🛡️ Win-WLAN-Audit-Tool.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: 📌 Project Overview The Win-WLAN-Audit-Tool is a specialized post-exploitation security script designed for Windows environments. It demonstrates the ease with which stored wireless credentials can be retrieved by an authorized user or an attacker who has gained local access to a system.
documentation.how_to_use: Clone or install `Win-WLAN-Audit-Tool` from its upstream repository, then run `Win-WLAN-Audit-Tool --help` (or the README entrypoint) to confirm flags.
